title: Business Breakfast | Stock Market News | May 26, 2026 || TV5 Money

recommendations.note: Buy on every decline; headed toward 1000-plus
recommendations.detail: His repeated conviction call: buy Amara Raja on every decline, the stock is on its way to 1000-plus in the coming quarter. He flagged the latest result as essentially flat once ~209 cr of other income (insurance claims of 186 cr for the 2023 Chittoor fire and 122 cr business-interruption) is stripped out. Stay invested and accumulate on dips.

recommendations.note: Near 52-wk high; results discounted, buy only on dips
recommendations.detail: Trading near its 52-week high, and auto-ancillary companies are doing well on better-than-expected results. But results are already discounted, so for fresh buying he would buy only when a decline comes rather than chase at current levels.

recommendations.note: Strong MNC engineering name; buy on a decline
recommendations.detail: A strong multinational engineering company, no doubt about it. Already near its 52-week high at 5,370. Strong summer or a weaker monsoon lifts demand for such companies, so it is a good bet when a decline comes.

recommendations.note: Heavy speculation; book profits, don't buy fresh here
recommendations.detail: Recently listed and has a lot of speculative activity; trade it carefully. If raw-material cost impact shows up, there are signs it could come back to 350-360. If you already hold some, book some profits, and don't buy fresh at this level.

recommendations.note: Sugar = trading sector; regulatory overhang, big returns unlikely now
recommendations.detail: Regulatory interference has become too much on both the ethanol and export sides, with exports temporarily banned to control inflation and ethanol offtake insufficient. The recent 1-1.5 month rally in ethanol-based sugar names was bubble-like and big returns are unlikely now. Sugar is a trading-only sector, not wealth-creating.

recommendations.note: Same sugar overhang; only a dark-horse counter
recommendations.detail: Same regulatory overhang as the broader sugar space, with exports banned and weak ethanol offtake. He sees it only as a dark-horse counter (there are better counters in sugar) and treats sugar as a sector to trade in and out of, not hold.

recommendations.note: Disinvestment of subsidiaries to lift stock over ~6 months
recommendations.detail: He would buy NTPC because two-three more subsidiaries are being prepared by the government for disinvestment. Over the next six months, either for that disinvestment or the gains it brings, the stock should rise. It is close to its 52-week high (414) at 391, hasn't fallen much, so stay invested and fresh buying can also be done.
